opp.name: Jazz_Pharma_ANA_June 26'
opp.next_step: June 15 Rick: Intro meeting drove interest to participate in ANA Pilot, and supporting Procurement modules. No plans to throw away Coupa as they are 18 months in, but prefers our solution and its modern digitization capabilities. Scope leaning towards Supplier/Risk, Sourcing, ANA, Intake, iSaaS
opp.ais_why:
• Jazz Pharmaceuticals is 'evaluating AI-driven procurement capabilities to enhance its existing Coupa environment,' with key focus areas including 'Autonomous Negotiation' and 'Third-Party Risk Management.'
• The full agentic suite (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is scoped, and a lean 25-person team managing ~$1.3B spend points to strong automation leverage on tail spend.
• AI is the explicit purpose of the evaluation rather than a bundled feature, supporting a hungry classification.

items.quote: All engagement single-threaded through Greg Fly (VP Procurement); no Decision Maker participation or identification in recent interactions

items.area: Economic Buyer confirmation and approval chain
items.quote: Philip Johnson (CFO) hypothesized as EB from prior mapping, but no recent confirmation of role, authority, or engagement; approval chain above Greg unknown

items.area: Formal decision criteria and evaluation process
items.quote: No RFP/RFI, BRD, weighted scorecard, or structured use-case validation documented; evaluation criteria inferred from Greg's questions but not formalized

items.area: Paper process: contracting, legal, InfoSec, ERP/systems integration
items.quote: Jazz is 18 months into Coupa deployment; integration with Coupa, data migration, and ERP/systems touchpoints undocumented; no MSA/SOW, legal review, or InfoSec gate discussed
No contract expiration, budget cycle deadline, or regulatory forcing function identified; urgency is pain-driven but not anchored to timeline
No mention of other vendors (Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer) being evaluated; unclear if this is Zycus-vs-Coupa sole-source or broader vendor selection

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-06-05: Greg Fly stated 30-40% underperformance vs benchmark, 25-person team, wants 50% supplier count reduction
Next Step 2026-06-15: Greg expressed interest in ANA pilot and procurement modules, indicating pain is driving urgency
metrics.narrative: Greg Fly articulated clear quantified pain on the intro call (2026-06-05): his 25-person procurement team is underperforming by 30-40% against benchmark, and he wants to reduce supplier count by 50%. He also cited tail-spend opportunity as a driver. These are concrete, measurable business problems with specific numerical targets. The pain is operational (team underperformance, supplier fragmentation) and financial (tail-spend leakage). Greg owns the pain and is the sponsor driving the evaluation. No formal ROI workshop or value case has been built yet, but the buyer has quantified the gap, which provides a strong foundation for later ROI discussions. The next step is to translate these metrics into a financial business case (cost savings, efficiency gains, headcount leverage) tied to the ANA and procurement module scope.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-06-05: Greg Fly drove the meeting, articulated pain, expressed interest in ANA and procurement modules
Next Step 2026-06-15: Greg expressed preference for Zycus over Coupa, requested ANA pilot demo within 1-2 weeks, asked for Horizon event details for his head of AP
Contact role: Greg Fly, VP Procurement, primary contact
champion.narrative: Greg Fly (VP Procurement) is the developing champion. He is the pain owner, drove the intro meeting, expressed clear preference for Zycus over Coupa, and proactively requested an ANA pilot demo with his COE team within 1-2 weeks (Next Step 2026-06-15). He also requested Zycus send Horizon event details to his head of AP, indicating he is beginning to socialize Zycus internally. However, Greg's championship is still developing: he has not yet introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, has not shared internal competitive intelligence (e.g. whether other vendors are being evaluated), and has not demonstrated active advocacy in a multi-vendor setting. His access to the CFO (Philip Johnson, likely EB) and approval chain is unconfirmed. Current engagement is active: he responded to Rick's outreach and set a clear near-term milestone (ANA pilot demo). The risk is that Greg can champion at the operational level but may not hold commercial sign-off authority for a $250k deal, and if he cannot navigate the approval chain, the deal could stall. The next step is to use the ANA pilot demo to deepen Greg's advocacy and ask him to introduce Zycus to the budget authority and decision maker.

sources: Next Step 2026-06-15: Jazz is 18 months into Coupa deployment, no plans to throw away Coupa, but Greg prefers Zycus solution and modern digitization capabilities
Intro call 2026-06-05: Greg expressed dissatisfaction with current state (30-40% underperformance), indicating Coupa is not meeting expectations
competition.narrative: Coupa is the confirmed competitor and incumbent. Jazz is 18 months into a Coupa deployment (Next Step 2026-06-15: 'No plans to throw away Coupa as they are 18 months in'), but Greg Fly expressed preference for Zycus's modern digitization capabilities over Coupa's approach. This creates a displacement or co-exist scenario: Jazz is unlikely to rip out Coupa entirely, so the wedge is to position Zycus as the modern, AI-led layer for ANA, sourcing, supplier/risk, and intake that complements or replaces underperforming Coupa modules. The how-we-win is: (1) lead with ANA autonomous negotiation as the differentiator Coupa cannot match, (2) emphasize modern user experience and digitization vs Coupa's legacy UX, (3) position Merlin agentic AI as the Phase 2 value unlock, (4) frame integration with Coupa as a strength (Zycus as the innovation layer on top of Coupa's transactional backbone). The risk is that Coupa has 18 months of sunk cost, data migration complexity, and organizational inertia. The next step is to use the ANA pilot demo to create a stark capability gap that makes staying with Coupa alone untenable, and to surface whether Jazz is evaluating other vendors (Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP) or this is a Zycus-vs-Coupa sole-source evaluation.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-06-05: Greg Fly stated 30-40% underperformance vs benchmark, 25-person team, 50% supplier count reduction goal, tail-spend opportunity
Next Step 2026-06-15: Greg prefers Zycus solution over Coupa despite 18-month Coupa deployment, indicating dissatisfaction with current state
identify_pain.narrative: Greg Fly (VP Procurement) is the pain owner and articulated specific operational and financial pain on the intro call (2026-06-05). His 25-person procurement team is underperforming by 30-40% against benchmark, he wants to reduce supplier count by 50%, and he cited tail-spend opportunity as a driver. He also expressed frustration with Coupa, stating he prefers Zycus's modern digitization capabilities despite being 18 months into the Coupa deployment (Next Step 2026-06-15). The pain is confirmed, quantified, and owned. It is operational (team underperformance, supplier fragmentation) and financial (tail-spend leakage, cost savings opportunity). The pain is current and active, not speculative. Greg's urgency is pain-driven, though not yet anchored to a forcing function (contract expiration, budget cycle, or regulatory deadline). The next step is to deepen the pain case by exploring: what is the financial impact of the 30-40% underperformance (lost savings, opportunity cost), and what is the timeline pressure (when does this pain become critical).

sources: Next Step 2026-06-15: Jazz is 18 months into Coupa deployment, no integration or paper process discussed
No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or SI involvement mentioned in any interaction
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, and implementation scoping are completely unmapped. Jazz is 18 months into a Coupa deployment (Next Step 2026-06-15: 'No plans to throw away Coupa'), which creates a significant integration and data-migration question: how will Zycus co-exist with or replace Coupa modules, what ERP/systems integration is required, and what InfoSec/IT review gates exist. None of this has been surfaced. There is no mention of MSA/SOW timing, legal redline process, or whether an SI/partner will be involved in implementation. No contract expiration or forcing function has been identified. The next step is to ask Greg during or after the ANA pilot demo: what is your contracting and legal review process, who handles vendor onboarding and InfoSec review, and how do you see Zycus integrating with your existing Coupa and ERP systems.

sources: Prior contact role mapping: Philip Johnson (CFO) listed as likely EB
Intro call 2026-06-05 and Next Step 2026-06-15: all engagement single-threaded through Greg Fly (VP Procurement), no EB participation recorded
economic_buyer.narrative: Philip Johnson (CFO) is likely the Economic Buyer based on prior contact role mapping, but commercial authority and approval chain have not been confirmed in recent interactions. Greg Fly (VP Procurement) is the operational sponsor and pain owner, but for a $250k deal in a regulated pharma company, CFO or C-level sign-off is standard. Greg has not yet surfaced the approval chain above him, and no EB/CFO has appeared on calls or in stakeholder discussions. The intro call (2026-06-05) and follow-up outreach (2026-06-15) have been single-threaded through Greg. This is a partial read: we have a hypothesis (Philip Johnson, CFO) from prior mapping, but no recent confirmation of his role, engagement, or authority. The next move is to ask Greg explicitly who holds budget authority and approval for this size deal, and to request an introduction or joint session with that person once the ANA pilot demo lands well.

sources: Intro call 2026-06-05: single-threaded through Greg Fly (VP Procurement)
Next Step 2026-06-15: Greg requested ANA pilot demo with his COE team within 1-2 weeks, indicating technical evaluation step
Zycus commitment to send Horizon event invitation to Greg's head of AP (Palo Alto), indicating lateral stakeholder engagement beginning
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is emerging but not yet mapped. The call sequence shows Greg Fly (VP Procurement) as the initial and only participant (intro call 2026-06-05), and he has requested an ANA pilot demo with his COE (center of excellence) team within 1-2 weeks (Next Step 2026-06-15), indicating a multi-person technical evaluation step is coming. Greg also asked Rick to send event invitation details for the Zycus Horizon conference to his head of AP (accounts payable) in Palo Alto, suggesting lateral stakeholder engagement is beginning. However, the approval chain above Greg is unmapped: who reviews the COE team's recommendation, who holds budget authority, whether procurement/InfoSec/legal/IT have formal gates, and whether this follows a standard vendor selection or RFP process is unknown. Greg has not surfaced a timeline beyond the near-term pilot demo request. The next step is to ask Greg explicitly: after the COE demo, what is the next gate, who reviews the results, and what is the approval path to contract.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-06-05: Greg asked about modular vs bundled platform, signaling pricing flexibility and scope as criteria; expressed preference for Zycus's modern digitization over Coupa
Next Step 2026-06-15: Greg requested ANA pilot demo, indicating autonomous AI negotiation is a key evaluation criterion
decision_criteria.narrative: Greg Fly has surfaced some evaluation criteria implicitly. On the intro call (2026-06-05) he asked whether the platform requires all modules or allows selective use, signaling that pricing flexibility and modular scope are decision factors. He also expressed preference for Zycus's modern digitization capabilities over Coupa's approach, indicating that user experience, platform modernity, and digital workflow are criteria. The ANA pilot request (Next Step 2026-06-15) suggests that autonomous AI negotiation capability is a key evaluation criterion, likely central to addressing the tail-spend and supplier-count reduction goals. However, formal decision criteria (weighted scorecard, RFP/RFI requirements, BRD fit, or structured use-case validation) have not been documented. The next step is to use the ANA pilot demo (requested within 1-2 weeks) to surface and confirm the full evaluation criteria: what Jazz will measure, how they will score vendors, who reviews the results, and what threshold defines success.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS field data available (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are null). However, buyer behavior indicates AI-hungry tier: Greg Fly proactively requested an ANA (autonomous negotiation agent) pilot demo within 1-2 weeks of the intro meeting, the deal is named after ANA, and the product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA. Greg's pain (tail-spend, supplier reduction) maps directly to ANA's value proposition. This is a strong AI fit signal from call content, even without AIS field scoring.

north_star_verdict.math: 177 days to close (2026-12-24). Deal created 2026-06-05, qualified immediately, 25 days old. Qualified stage expected duration for enterprise deals is 60-90 days before progressing to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted. Current pace: 25 days in Qualified is within normal band. No forward slip recorded. Required pace: must reach Formal Evaluation by mid-August and Shortlisted by October to support year-end close. Submission-date series unavailable (Qualified_Submission_Date__c and subsequent milestones unread), so velocity computed from stage entry (CreatedDate 2026-06-05). LastActivityDate 2026-06-29 confirms recent engagement. Next Step dated 2026-06-15 shows rep-initiated outreach with clear buyer interest articulated (ANA pilot, procurement module support, preference for Zycus over Coupa). No stall signals. Math supports On Track.

Single-threaded risk: zero buyer-side contact roles, only Greg Fly engaged. Must multi-thread into Greg's center of excellence team and GPLT during the upcoming ANA demo to de-risk champion dependency and build consensus across the buying committee.

Power-role gap: Economic Buyer and Decision Maker unmapped. Greg holds VP Procurement title and $1.314B spend authority but likely needs CFO or C-suite sign-off for a $250k platform deal. Use the COE demo to ask Greg about his approval chain and request an intro to the commercial decision-maker.

Overdue deliverables blocking next step: Zycus committed to send meeting slides, video recording, and Horizon event details by ~12 June; both are overdue by 14 days. Greg committed to send his availability for the COE demo by ~19 June; also overdue by 7 days. Deliver the materials and re-engage Greg immediately to unlock the pilot demo, the single highest-leverage next step.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: No plans to throw away Coupa as they are 18 months in, but prefers our solution and its modern digitization capabilities.
value.how_we_win: Lead with ANA autonomous negotiation as the differentiator Coupa cannot match; emphasize modern UX and digitization vs Coupa legacy; position Merlin agentic AI as Phase 2 value unlock; frame integration with Coupa as strength (Zycus as innovation layer on top of Coupa transactional backbone). Use ANA pilot demo to create stark capability gap.
